reasoning_text: Architecture A (MCP-tool-direct-Perplexity-API) was selected over B (Comet Bridge sibling) and C (hybrid) for the Pawn-via-Librarian dispatch channel. Rationale: (1) K499 empirically proved PERPLEXITY_API_KEY works at API layer (len=53, 8/8 models pass); (2) API path inlines prompt content — no local file paths ever reach Perplexity's browser-sandboxed environment, permanently closing the ERR_FILE_NOT_FOUND failure class; (3) per-call cost $0.07-0.15 per K528 anchor is well within the $1.00 per-dispatch hard cap; (4) audit-by-default: every dispatch creates dispatch record JSON + return JSON + ledger entry + telemetry entry. Architecture B (browser extension) remains documented as a fallback if API cost proves unacceptable post-telemetry.
cited_facts: K499: PERPLEXITY_API_KEY len=53, API verified
K528: sonar-pro $0.239/HOT at 200-question scale (cost anchor)
K530: Comet Bridge generalization at perplexity.ai surface (B fallback basis)

reasoning_text: PAWN_VIA_LIBRARIAN_DISPATCH_ENABLED=false ships as the committed default in config/pawn_dispatch_caps.json. This is the same mechanical-publication-gate pattern as K525 (Conductor's Baton shipped with production=false; Founder flipped after Stage 3 integration). The tool returns a structured error_class='feature_flag_off' immediately when the gate is off — zero API calls, zero cost. Founder flips to true after Phase E validation lands AND ledger discipline is confirmed (both must be satisfied). This prevents accidental Pawn dispatches from Bishop sessions before the channel is fully operationally validated.
cited_facts: K525: production_mode=false mechanical gate; same pattern
K532 config: PAWN_VIA_LIBRARIAN_DISPATCH_ENABLED=false
K532 error: feature_flag_off returned with no API call when gate is off

reasoning_text: Two-tier cost cap: (1) per-dispatch hard ceiling $1.00 (K528 anchor: largest Pawn research dispatch Keirsey validation = $0.072 actual estimate; ceiling is 14x safety margin); (2) daily ceiling $10.00 (configured in pawn_dispatch_caps.json). Pre-call estimation uses token approximation (input chars / 4) + pricing from PRICING_USD_PER_1M table matching K528's perplexity_spaces_adapter.py. If pre-call estimate exceeds per-dispatch cap, tool returns cost_cap_exceeded with requires_founder_authorization=true WITHOUT calling the API. Daily spend is tracked from telemetry/pawn_dispatch_costs.jsonl — only cost_actual_usd entries for today's ISO date are summed.
cited_facts: K528 Keirsey prompt estimate: $0.072144 (4048 input tokens, 4000 output tokens)
sonar-pro pricing: $3/M input, $15/M output
K525 costCap.ts pattern reused conceptually

reasoning_text: Every Pawn dispatch creates three artifacts: (1) dispatches/pawn/<dispatch_id>.dispatch.json — full mutable dispatch record updated through lifecycle (initiated → dispatched/error/cancelled); (2) dispatches/pawn/<dispatch_id>.return.json — immutable response record written once on success; (3) append-only entry in dispatches/pawn/dispatch_ledger.jsonl — snapshot of record at terminal state. Telemetry entry also appended to telemetry/pawn_dispatch_costs.jsonl for daily spend tracking. The dispatch record carries: dispatch_id (UUID), prompt_hash (SHA-256), response_hash (SHA-256), cost_estimate_usd, cost_actual_usd, model, all timestamps, dispatch_metadata, attempt_log. This supports Bishop queries: 'all dispatches in session B130', 'all dispatches for cohort=Keirsey', 'any duplicate prompts', 'total daily spend'.
cited_facts: K532 ledger entry: dispatch_id=6fcb4451, prompt_hash=639b0dbe..., cost_estimate_usd=0.072144

reasoning_text: Phase C.10 integration test empirically hit the K528/TS-108 pattern: Perplexity account quota exhausted (HTTP 401) during live dispatch of Keirsey research prompt. Tool correctly returned error_class='quota_exhausted' + requires_founder_credit_topup=true without leaking any key or retrying indefinitely. Ledger entry captured the attempt_log: '[dispatch_initiated] → [attempt 1/5] → [HTTP 401 — quota exhausted]'. This is NOT an architectural failure — it's a billing-account state. Resume pattern: Founder tops up at perplexity.ai/settings/api; then dispatch_pawn() with same prompt will succeed. The dispatch record (6fcb4451) is in the ledger as error/quota_exhausted. Founder should create a new dispatch (fresh dispatch_id) after top-up, not attempt to resume the errored one.
cited_facts: TS-108: Perplexity quota exhaustion pattern from K528
K532 dispatch_id=6fcb4451 status=error error_class=quota_exhausted
Cost estimate before 401: $0.072144 (no actual spend — API refused before billing)

reasoning_text: The critical design choice that closes the ERR_FILE_NOT_FOUND failure class: dispatch_pawn() takes prompt_content (full text, required) NOT a file path. Pawn (Perplexity-in-browser) is filesystem-sandboxed — it cannot resolve local file paths (e.g., C:\Users\...\PAWN_KEIRSEY.md). By inlining the content in the API POST request body, the dispatch is completely sandboxed from the filesystem. The prompt_artifact_path parameter is optional and is used ONLY for ledger record purposes — it never leaves the Knight-side dispatch system. This is the architectural fix for the Founder-reported ERR_FILE_NOT_FOUND failure that triggered B130A and K531/K532.
cited_facts: B130A: Founder dispatch via local-file-path reference failed ERR_FILE_NOT_FOUND
Architecture A: prompt_content (string) in API request body; prompt_artifact_path for ledger only

reasoning_text: All Pawn dispatches live entirely in the Cathedral substrate's audit/ledger (librarian-mcp/dispatches/pawn/, librarian-mcp/telemetry/). They are never published, never cited externally, never attributed to letter recipients. The dispatch_metadata field carries session_id, cohort, and founder_authorized flag — Bishop-side provenance for internal research classification. This honors Three-Class Substrate Sovereignty (A&A #2315): Pawn dispatches are Class 1 (internal research), not Class 2 (member-facing content) or Class 3 (public-facing content). The separation is structural: dispatches/ and telemetry/ directories are gitignored (runtime state); only the tool code (pawn_dispatch.ts) and config (pawn_dispatch_caps.json) are committed.
cited_facts: A&A #2315: Three-Class Substrate Sovereignty
librarian-mcp/.gitignore includes dispatches/ — runtime state not committed
dispatch_metadata.cohort='Keirsey-validation' — internal research label

reasoning_text: pawn_dispatch.ts implements 5-retry exponential backoff matching K528's perplexity_adapter.py pattern. 401 (quota exhausted) is a hard stop — no retry, immediate return with quota_exhausted error. 429 (rate-limited) parses Retry-After header (handling both float seconds and Xm Ys formats) and waits, then retries. Other HTTP errors (4xx/5xx) do exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s (capped at 30s for 429). Network errors (fetch throws) also retry with same backoff. All attempts logged to attempt_log[] in the dispatch record for forensic analysis. This matches TS-108's documented resume pattern for Perplexity API quota issues.
cited_facts: K528 perplexity_adapter.py: max 5 retries, exponential backoff, Retry-After header parse
TS-108: Retry-After values can escalate to 14+ minutes for rate-limited endpoints

reasoning_text: expected_return_path is resolved relative to workspace root (librarian-mcp/../) when not absolute. This means Bishop can pass 'BISHOP_DROPZONE/02_PawnPrompts/PAWN_RETURN_KEIRSEY_RESEARCH_LETTER_RECIPIENTS_B130.md' and the file lands at C:\Users\Administrator\Documents\LianaBanyanPlatform\BISHOP_DROPZONE\02_PawnPrompts\PAWN_RETURN_KEIRSEY_RESEARCH_LETTER_RECIPIENTS_B130.md — exactly where the standard Read tool can find it. The parent directory is created if it doesn't exist (ensureDir). The return file content is the raw Perplexity API response text. Bishop absorbs this via the standard Read tool, consistent with E.4 spec.
cited_facts: expected_return_path resolution: workspace_root = resolve(LIBRARIAN_ROOT, '..')
mkdirSync(returnDir, {recursive: true}) ensures parent dir exists

reasoning_text: K532 implements duplicate prompt detection via SHA-256 hash of prompt_content, checked against existing ledger entries. If a match is found, the tool appends a duplicate_advisory field to the result (NOT an error — re-validation is valid). Bishop can see 'duplicate_prompt_detected: prompt_hash X matches dispatch_id Y from timestamp T' and decide whether to proceed. This reinforces Bishop discipline: don't re-dispatch the same research prompt unless you have a reason (e.g., Pawn credit refill changed the result, or you want a second opinion). The advisory preserves audit trail of repeated dispatches.
cited_facts: SHA-256 prompt_hash in dispatch record
duplicate_advisory field in PawnDispatchResult — advisory only, not error

reasoning_text: K532 exposes four MCP tools: (1) dispatch_pawn — primary tool, takes prompt_content inline, returns dispatch_id; (2) check_pawn_dispatch — poll for status by dispatch_id; (3) cancel_pawn_dispatch — mark a dispatch as cancelled (does not abort in-flight HTTP); (4) list_pending_pawn_dispatches — list recent dispatch records with status/cost summary. The four-tool surface matches B.1/B.6/B.7 spec. All four register via the existing registerTool() wrapper in server.ts which applies build-gate check + session telemetry tracking (K506 pattern). No new dependencies added to package.json — uses Node.js built-in fetch (Node 22), built-in crypto (SHA-256), built-in fs.
cited_facts: server.ts registerTool() pattern: K448 build-gate + K506 telemetry tracking
Node.js 22: fetch global available (no openai/axios dependency needed)
4 tools: dispatch_pawn, check_pawn_dispatch, cancel_pawn_dispatch, list_pending_pawn_dispatches

reasoning_text: K532 composes with three predecessors: (1) K530 Comet Bridge — Architecture B fallback path; K530's lb-omnibox-extension/ demonstrates the browser-injection approach that would be the basis for an lb-pawn-bridge-extension/ if API cost proves unacceptable; (2) K525 Conductor's Baton — mechanical publication gate pattern reused (feature_flag_off return, per-call cost cap discipline, telemetry append-only log); (3) K528 R11-v2 Full-Stack — provides the cost anchor ($0.239/HOT = ~$0.07-0.15 per bounded research dispatch), the quota_exhausted resume pattern (TS-108), and the Perplexity API integration proof. K532 closes the Pawn validation deferral that began when the B130A Founder dispatch attempt returned ERR_FILE_NOT_FOUND.
cited_facts: K530: lb-omnibox-extension/ proves Comet Bridge pattern
K525: publication gate feature_flag pattern
K528: $0.072144 Keirsey dispatch cost estimate, quota_exhausted pattern documented

The channel is now architectural (durable tool), not workaround (paste-and-go).
